parsed.diagnosis: This job performs exclusively small POSIX write operations of 64 bytes each, resulting in a very high small I/O ratio (100%) and a very low average write size (64 bytes). Such tiny writes cause excessive system call overhead and metadata operations (metadata time ratio ~19%), severely limiting I/O bandwidth to about 29 MB/s. The sequential write pattern indicates no randomness, so aggregating writes is feasible and beneficial.

recommendations.explanation: The job issues many small POSIX write calls (64 bytes each), causing excessive syscall overhead and metadata operations. Aggregating these small writes into larger buffers (>=1MB) before issuing a single write reduces syscall frequency and improves throughput.
recommendations.code_before: for (i=0; i<n; i++) write(fd, buf, 64);  /* 64B per call */
recommendations.expected_speedup: Up to 3-5x based on KB entries ior_ior_small_posix_t64_n4_r1_0, ior_ior_small_posix_t64_n4_r3_2, ior_ior_small_posix_t64_n4_r2_1
recommendations.api_change: POSIX write with small sizes -> POSIX write with large buffered chunks

parsed.overall_expected_improvement: 3x to 5x total speedup by reducing syscall overhead and metadata time through write aggregation
